briefdescription.para: Underlying composite IDs that represent a full AccelByte UniqueNetId.
Flag denoting whether the validity of this ID has already been checked.
Cached state of this ID's validity.
Create a new AccelByte User UniqueNetId instance from a composite ID.

parameterdescription.para: The AccelByte User UniqueNetId in composite structure.

simplesect.para: A shared reference of AccelByte User UniqueNetId object.

briefdescription.para: Create a new AccelByte User UniqueNetId instance from a string of UniqueNetId.

simplesect.para: A shared reference of AccelByte User UniqueNetId object.

briefdescription.para: Create a new AccelByte User UniqueNetId instance from a generic UniqueNetId.

simplesect.para: A shared reference of AccelByte User UniqueNetId object.

briefdescription.para: Takes an UniqueNetId object and converts it to an AccelByte User UniqueNetId instance if the type matches. Will return an Invalid ID reference if the conversion cannot be made. Prefer the other Cast methods if possible.

parameterdescription.para: The UniqueNetId object to attempt to convert to an AccelByte User UniqueNetId instance.

simplesect.para: A shared reference of AccelByte User UniqueNetId object.

briefdescription.para: Attempts to convert an UniqueNetId object to an AccelByte User UniqueNetId instance. Will return nullptr if the cast cannot be made.

parameterdescription.para: The UniqueNetId object to attempt to convert to an AccelByte User UniqueNetId instance.

simplesect.para: A shared pointer of AccelByte User UniqueNetId object.

briefdescription.para: Attempts to convert a shared reference of UniqueNetId object to an AccelByte User UniqueNetId instance. Will return nullptr if the cast cannot be made.

parameterdescription.para: The shared reference of UniqueNetId object to attempt to convert to an AccelByte User UniqueNetId instance.

simplesect.para: A shared pointer of AccelByte User UniqueNetId object.

briefdescription.para: Convenience method to construct an invalid instance of an AccelByte UniqueNetId.

simplesect.para: A shared reference of AccelByte User UniqueNetId object that has Invalid ID.

briefdescription.para: Attempts to convert an UniqueNetId object to an AccelByte User UniqueNetId instance. Guarded by a check call that will crash if an improper ID is attempted to be casted.

parameterdescription.para: The UniqueNetId object that will be converted to AccelByte User UniqueNetId instance.

simplesect.para: A shared reference of AccelByte User UniqueNetId object.

briefdescription.para: Attempts to convert a shared reference of UniqueNetId object to an AccelByte User UniqueNetId instance. Guarded by a check call that will will crash if an improper ID is attempted to be casted.

parameterdescription.para: The shared reference of UniqueNetId object that will be converted to AccelByte User UniqueNetId instance.

simplesect.para: A shared reference of AccelByte User UniqueNetId object.

briefdescription.para: Return the type of this unique ID. In this case, it should always be ACCELBYTE_USER_ID_TYPE.
Whether or not this ID is a valid AccelByte UniqueNetId type. Will do a number of checks including:

listitem.para: Whether the underlying string value is Base64
Whether upon decoding the string value there is a JSON object with id, platformType, and platformId fields
Whether the id field in the JSON object is in the correct format for an AccelByte ID

briefdescription.para: Returns the JSON representation of AccelByte composite ID, useful for debugging.

simplesect.para: a string of JSON representation of the composite ID.

briefdescription.para: Get the string representation of the AccelByte user ID from the composite ID.
Get the string representation of the type of platform for platform ID from the composite ID.

simplesect.para: a string representation of the type of platform.

briefdescription.para: Get the string representation of the ID of the user for the platform type specified from the composite ID.
Checks whether or not this composite ID has platform information included.

simplesect.para: true if composite ID has platform information and false if no platform information presents.

briefdescription.para: Tries to convert the platform ID information into that native platform OSS' unique ID type.

simplesect.para: A pointer to Native Platform UniqueNetId.

briefdescription.para: Gets the underlying composite structure for this unique ID.

simplesect.para: AccelByte Unique ID in composite structure.

briefdescription.para: Override equal check operator to check the AccelByte ID first, and then the platform type/ID.

simplesect.para: true if both object has the same ID and false if not the same

briefdescription.para: Method that will decode a given string from Base64 into the correct ID format, as well as fill out necessary fields.
Default constructor.
Internal constructor that accepts an UniqueNetId string.
Internal constructor that pass the ownership of UniqueNetId string.
Internal constructor that accepts an instance of UniqueNetId.
Internal constructor that accepts an UniqueNetId string and its type name.
Internal constructor that accepts the ownership of UniqueNetId string and its type name.

Unique IDs to be used by the AccelByte online subsystem for users. This implementation follows a composite structure, where we not only store the AccelByte ID in a single structure, but also, optionally, the platform name and ID in the same structure.

detaileddescription.para: These IDs can be casted to and broken apart to get those IDs as needed, such as for interop with native platform SDKs.

For this, there will be three extra fields in an FUniqueNetIdAccelByte, which are as follows:
AccelByteIDString
PlatformTypeString
PlatformIDString

When any of these are set, the underlying string for FUniqueNetIdString is set to a Base64 encoded JSON object that contains all of these fields individually. An example of this JSON object is as follows: { "id": "<AccelByte ID>", "platformType": "<type of platform the platformId field corresponds to, can be blank>", "platformId": "<ID of the platform that platformType corresponds to, can be blank>" }
ToString will return the encoded version of this string, while ToDebugString will return the decoded version.

To get any of these fields from the ID, you will want to cast to an FUniqueNetIdAccelByte and use the getters there. However, most of the ID manipulation is handled for you by the OSS, so there shouldn't be a need to crack open these IDs unless you need to make SDK calls yourself.
